title: osmanthus ilicifolius f. aureus
intro: Osmanthus ilicifolius f. aureus, also known as Golden Tea Olive, is a cultivar of the Osmanthus ilicifolius plant, a flowering evergreen shrub native to China. This plant is prized for its beautiful golden foliage and fragrant white flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments. The leaves and flowers of Osmanthus ilicifolius f. aureus contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are believed to possess medicinal properties that can help to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other health problems.
